commands.review_summary:
The PR changes package-manager detection and npm dependency-status checks so published-package roots with `npm-shrinkwrap.json` are treated as npm-managed when `pnpm-lock.yaml` is absent, with focused regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +21, Tests +36. Total +57 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main reads `packageManager` before lockfile markers, so the reported npm-installed package shape is classified as pnpm. The linked issue also provides live status output from OpenClaw 2026.5.27 showing the same failure mode.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; the patch looks correct and the remaining action is maintainer acceptance of the compatibility boundary plus normal merge checks.

target.title: fix(infra): prefer npm-shrinkwrap.json over packageManager field for npm detection
